1276     # page, so the non-sargable filename LIKE runs once, not twice (NF3).
1277     ranked_sql = f"""
1278     WITH raw_matches AS (
1279         SELECT f.id, f.file_name, f.vault_id, f.status, f.parsed_text,
1280                bm25(files_content_fts) AS rank,
1281                highlight(files_content_fts, 0, '<mark>', '</mark>') AS excerpt_raw,
1282                1 AS type_priority
1283         FROM files_content_fts
1284         JOIN files f ON f.id = files_content_fts.rowid
1285         WHERE files_content_fts MATCH ?
1286           AND {vault_clause_body}
1287         UNION ALL
1288         SELECT f.id, f.file_name, f.vault_id, f.status, f.parsed_text,
1289                bm25(files_search_fts) AS rank,
1290                COALESCE(
1291                    NULLIF(highlight(files_search_fts, 4, '<mark>', '</mark>'), ''),
1292                    NULLIF(highlight(files_search_fts, 5, '<mark>', '</mark>'), ''),
1293                    NULLIF(highlight(files_search_fts, 1, '<mark>', '</mark>'), ''),
1294                    NULLIF(highlight(files_search_fts, 3, '<mark>', '</mark>'), ''),
1295                    NULLIF(highlight(files_search_fts, 6, '<mark>', '</mark>'), ''),
1296                    highlight(files_search_fts, 0, '<mark>', '</mark>')
1297                ) AS excerpt_raw,
1298                2 AS type_priority
1299         FROM files_search_fts
1300         JOIN files f ON f.id = files_search_fts.rowid
1301         WHERE files_search_fts MATCH ?
1302           AND {vault_clause_meta}
1303         UNION ALL
1304         SELECT f.id, f.file_name, f.vault_id, f.status, f.parsed_text,
1305                0.0 AS rank, NULL AS excerpt_raw,
1306                3 AS type_priority
1307         FROM files f
1308         WHERE LOWER(f.file_name) LIKE ?
1309           AND {vault_clause_name}
1310     ),
1311     ranked AS (
1312         SELECT id, file_name, vault_id, status, rank, excerpt_raw, parsed_text, type_priority,
1313                ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY id ORDER BY type_priority ASC, rank ASC) AS rn
1314         FROM raw_matches
1315     ),
1316     deduped AS (
1317         SELECT id, file_name, vault_id, status, rank, excerpt_raw, parsed_text, type_priority
1318         FROM ranked WHERE rn = 1
1319     ),
1320     full_count AS (
1321         SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM deduped
1322     )
1323     SELECT d.id, d.file_name, d.vault_id, d.status, d.rank, d.excerpt_raw,
1324            d.parsed_text, d.type_priority, fc.n AS total_count
1325     FROM deduped d, full_count fc
1326     ORDER BY d.type_priority ASC, d.rank ASC
1327     LIMIT ? OFFSET ?
1328     """
1329
